basics.name: Zhi Yang
basics.label: Embodied AI Researcher
basics.summary: M.Eng. student in Artificial Intelligence at Beijing Institute of Technology, specializing in Embodied AI, Vision-Language Navigation, and Multi-Agent Systems.

highlights: Research: Embodied Navigation, VLN, LLM-driven Autonomous Exploration

education.institution: City University of Hong Kong (QS Top 50)

awards.title: China Robotics & AI Competition - First Prize
awards.summary: First Prize at national robotics and artificial intelligence competition

publications.name: InfoNav: A Unified Value Framework Integrating Semantics Value and Information Gain for Navigation
publications.summary: A unified value framework for embodied navigation integrating semantic value and information gain. Features Fast-Slow dual-system architecture with 20x speedup.

projects.name: InfoNav: VLM-driven Embodied Navigation
projects.summary: Zero-shot Object Navigation system for indoor environments. Features Fast-Slow dual-system architecture (20x speedup), LLM Agent decision module with DeepSeek-V3, and VLM semantic perception with Qwen3-VL/BLIP2.
highlights: Validated on HM3D/MP3D and real robots

projects.name: PaperTrack-Agent
projects.summary: Multi-agent research assistant system built with LangGraph + LLM + Redis + MySQL. Features automated paper retrieval, parsing, trend analysis, and research direction generation with ChromaDB for semantic search.

projects.name: Multimodal Vision-Language Navigation
projects.summary: Transformer-based VLN system with BERT text encoding, CLIP visual features, and depth information fusion. Implements topological planning with NetworkX for graph-based shortest path navigation.

projects.name: Air-Ground Heterogeneous Indoor Navigation
projects.summary: National Key R&D Program: Semantic-driven autonomous UAV exploration for post-earthquake damaged buildings. Built complete embodied navigation pipeline: Semantic Occupancy Mapping - LLM Agent Planning - MPC Control.

certificates.name: Patent: High-Precision 3D Semantic Map Construction Method
